form_data.Idea Name: AI-Driven Public Health Outbreak Early Warning System
form_data.Expected benefits:
Early detection of disease outbreaks

Faster public health interventions

Reduced mortality and spread of infectious diseases

Data-driven allocation of medical resources
form_data.Idea Description:
This project focuses on developing an AI-based early disease outbreak detection platform using real-time health records, hospital admission logs, environmental data, and population mobility patterns. The system identifies unusual spikes in symptoms, predicts potential outbreak zones, and alerts healthcare authorities.

It integrates data from hospitals, wearable devices, pathology labs, and public health surveillance systems. Machine learning models detect anomalies, while geospatial analytics create hotspot maps for rapid response.
form_data.potentail Challenges:
Integrating sensitive health data across multiple hospitals and labs

Ensuring compliance with data protection laws (DPDP Act)

Handling incomplete or noisy health record data

Maintaining real-time performance during health emergencies
